taxonomy: Paratropia obtusa is classified as a species of spider in the family Barychelidae, which is a group of mygalomorph spiders. These spiders are commonly known as velvet worms due to their velvety appearance. Barychelidae spiders are typically found in tropical regions of the world and are characterized by their elongated bodies and hairy legs. The classification of Paratropia obtusa at a higher taxonomic level includes it within the order Araneomorphae, which comprises most modern spiders. At the kingdom level, Paratropia obtusa is classified as Animalia.
